title: crateva tapia subsp. odora
intro: Crateva tapia subsp. odora, also known as the fragrant crateva, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its fragrant flowers and edible fruits. The medicinal properties of Crateva tapia subsp. odora include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The health benefits of consuming Crateva tapia subsp. odora include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve respiratory health, and provide antioxidant properties. Crateva tapia subsp. odora can be prepared as a herbal tea or decoction by infusing the leaves or flowers in hot water, or as a tincture by soaking the plant parts in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin.
